food_subgroup: Stuffed savoury pancake with egg and chicken filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for martabak telur. The closest parent is the general pancake food product class. A more specific Indonesian stuffed pancake term is absent from FoodOn as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes) is the closest defensible parent. No specific martabak or Indonesian stuffed savoury pancake code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2026.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for martabak telur or Indonesian stuffed pancake as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Martabak Telur Ayam is 6 large pieces, weighing approximately 200 g, as stated by the restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Martabak Telur (Egg Martabak) — FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for egg-and-chicken pan-fried pancake analogues
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for martabak telur ayam exists in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA databases. Values are estimated by combining: (1) FAO/INFOODS SEA data for Indonesian egg-based savoury pancake analogues, (2) USDA SR Legacy data for pan-fried egg dishes and minced chicken, and (3) published nutrient composition studies for martabak telur in Indonesian food literature. The serving is 200 g (6 large pieces) per restaurant description. Per-100 g values are derived from this composite estimate.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of savoury egg pancake and analogous stuffed wheat-dough products — University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for martabak telur ayam. GI of 52 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for savoury egg-and-wheat pancake analogues (GI range 47–57, mean ~52). The thin wheat-flour wrapper and egg-dominant filling are consistent with a low-to-medium GI profile. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 33.0) / 100 = 17.2, where carbohydrate per 200 g serving = 16.5 × 2 = 33.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~760 mg per 200 g serving) from seasoned filling, sauce, and sambal
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid martabak telur ayam due to its high sodium load (~760 mg per serving), which represents approximately one-third of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; consuming without the accompanying sauce and sambal can meaningfully reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~17 per serving) from wheat-flour wrapper and moderate carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat martabak telur ayam as a moderate-GL food (GL ~17 per 200 g serving); portion control to 3 pieces (~100 g) and pairing with non-starchy vegetables can help attenuate postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~6.4 g per serving) and elevated cholesterol (~370 mg per serving) from egg-rich filling and pan-frying in oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit martabak telur ayam to occasional consumption given its moderate saturated fat content and high dietary cholesterol from eggs; choosing a smaller portion and avoiding the high-sodium sambal sauce further reduces cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Martabak Telur Ayam (Indonesian Chicken Egg Martabak) is a beloved Indonesian street food consisting of a thin, crispy wheat-flour wrapper pan-fried in oil and stuffed with a savoury filling of minced chicken, beaten eggs, spring onion, and leek. The exterior is golden and crisp while the interior remains moist and egg-rich. It is traditionally served with acar (a lightly pickled cucumber and carrot relish), a sweet-savoury martabak dipping sauce, and sambal chilli. Martabak telur is widely sold at street stalls and night markets across Indonesia and has become popular in Singapore through Indonesian restaurants. A standard serving of 6 large pieces weighs approximately 200 g. Nutritionally, each 100 g provides approximately 218 kcal, 11.5 g protein, 12 g fat, and 16.5 g carbohydrate. The dish is notable for its high egg content, which contributes significant dietary cholesterol (~185 mg per 100 g) and protein. Sodium content is elevated (~380 mg per 100 g) due to seasoning and accompanying sauces. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI) with a moderate glycemic load of approximately 17 per full serving, making portion control important for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans and contains gluten and egg allergens. It is generally considered halal when prepared with chicken and no pork-derived ingredients.
common_user_queries: How many calories in martabak chicken 6 pieces?
What is the GI of martabak?
What is the difference between martabak and murtabak?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for martabak telur ayam was found in FAO/INFOODS SEA, USDA FoodData Central, or HPB Singapore Foodo databases. Nutrient values are estimated from composite analogues (Indonesian egg-based savoury pancake data from FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA SR Legacy pan-fried egg and chicken entries). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for savoury egg-wheat pancake products. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA SR Legacy analogue sources used for this estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the analogue sources. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the analogue sources. All other -1 fields reflect genuine absence of data in the approved source analogues rather than confirmed zero values. Serving size of 200 g (6 large pieces) is taken from the restaurant description in the input record. The dish is sold at Smile Martabak - Funan in Singapore at SGD 9.90. The field survey source URL is preserved as contextual metadata only and was not used as a nutritional evidence source. Halal status is assumed based on chicken filling and Indonesian Muslim street food tradition but has not been independently verified for this specific restaurant.
